dialogues.title: Festa no Bairro
dialogues.context: A neighborhood festa in a local bar in Madeira. Friends are celebrating a small victory.

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: Então, malta! Um brinde à nossa equipa, que conseguiu o primeiro lugar no torneio de sueca!
lines.translation: Alright, folks! A toast to our team, who got first place in the 'sueca' tournament!

lines.speaker: Tiago
lines.text: Pois é! Bem merecido! Saúde a todos!
lines.translation: That's right! Well deserved! Cheers everyone!

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.text: Mais uma rodada de poncha para celebrar?
lines.translation: Another round of poncha to celebrate?

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: Claro! E que venham mais vitórias! Estou a ver que a noite vai ser longa!
lines.translation: Of course! And may more victories come! I can see it's going to be a long night!

lines.speaker: Tiago
lines.text: Ah, e a propósito, Lena, conseguiste arranjar aqueles bilhetes p'ra o concerto da próxima semana?
lines.translation: Ah, and by the way, Lena, did you manage to get those tickets for next week's concert?

dialogues.title: Notícias no Café
dialogues.context: At a local café, two older neighbours are discussing recent events over coffee.

lines.speaker: Sr. Manuel
lines.text: Bom dia, D. Rosa. Já ouviu falar daquele problema com a levada nova lá p'ra cima?
lines.translation: Good morning, Mrs. Rosa. Have you heard about that problem with the new levada up there?

lines.speaker: D. Rosa
lines.text: Diga? Não ouvi bem... O café tá uma barulheira hoje. Qual levada?
lines.translation: Say that again? I didn't hear well... The café is so noisy today. Which levada?

lines.speaker: Sr. Manuel
lines.text: A que estão a construir perto da serra. Dizem que há uns atrasos e desvios no orçamento.
lines.translation: The one they're building near the mountain. They say there are some delays and budget overruns.

lines.speaker: D. Rosa
lines.text: Ah, essa! Pois é, o meu genro já me tinha falado nisso. É uma vergonha, não é? Sempre a mesma história...
lines.translation: Ah, that one! Indeed, my son-in-law had already told me about it. It's a disgrace, isn't it? Always the same story...

lines.speaker: Sr. Manuel
lines.text: Exato. E com o dinheiro que aquilo vai custar, podiam era arranjar as estradas da nossa zona que estão uma lástima.
lines.translation: Exactly. And with the money that's going to cost, they could fix the roads in our area, which are a disaster.

cultural_notes.title: Toasting Etiquette in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: When making a toast ('um brinde'), it's customary to make eye contact with everyone whose glass you clink, or at least with the person you're toasting. You should also wait until everyone has clinked glasses before taking your first sip. Common toasts include 'Saúde!' (Cheers/To health!) or 'À nossa!' (To us!). At informal gatherings, just 'Saúde!' is perfectly acceptable.

cultural_notes.title: Condolence Visits: Sensitivity and Support
cultural_notes.body: Visiting someone who has lost a loved one is a delicate social situation. It's important to express genuine sympathy ('Os meus pêsames' or 'Sinto muito pela sua perda') and offer practical support ('Se precisar de alguma coisa, avise'). Keep the visit relatively brief, unless invited to stay longer. Avoid asking intrusive questions about the cause of death or dwelling on sad memories unless the bereaved person initiates it. The goal is to show support and respect for their grief, not to solve their problems or make them feel worse. A simple, heartfelt presence is often appreciated most.

roleplay.scenario: You are visiting your neighbour, D. Laura, whose sister recently passed away. You want to offer your condolences and support.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, bom dia. Entre, entre. Obrigada por ter vindo.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, good morning. Come in, come in. Thank you for coming.

options.text: D. Laura, os meus pêsames pela sua perda. Sinto imenso.
options.translation: D. Laura, my condolences for your loss. I'm terribly sorry.
options.feedback: This is a direct and appropriate expression of sympathy. Good start.

options.text: Bom dia, D. Laura. Como está a correr tudo? Não a via há um tempo.
options.translation: Good morning, D. Laura. How is everything going? I haven't seen you in a while.
options.feedback: While polite, this avoids the reason for your visit and might seem insensitive given the circumstances.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, pois... Como é que havia de estar? A minha irmã partiu há uma semana. É difícil, sabe?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, well... How do you think I'd be? My sister passed away a week ago. It's hard, you know?

options.text: Peço desculpa, D. Laura. Não queria ser insensível. Os meus pêsames, a sério.
options.translation: I'm sorry, D. Laura. I didn't mean to be insensitive. My sincere condolences.
options.feedback: Good recovery. Acknowledging your mistake and offering sincere condolences is important.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigada. Ela era uma pessoa muito especial. Deixa muitas saudades. Quer um café?
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you. She was a very special person. She'll be greatly missed. Would you like a coffee?

options.text: Sim, por favor. Se precisar de alguma coisa, seja para as compras ou para o que for, avise-me. Estou aqui para ajudar.
options.translation: Yes, please. If you need anything, whether for shopping or whatever, let me know. I'm here to help.
options.feedback: Excellent. You've accepted her offer and made a concrete, helpful offer in return.

options.text: Não, obrigada. Só queria passar para deixar os meus sentimentos. Tenho de ir.
options.translation: No, thank you. I just wanted to stop by to offer my condolences. I have to go.
options.feedback: While brief visits are appropriate, declining hospitality and rushing off might seem a bit abrupt, though still acceptable.

nodes.npc_text: É muito amável da sua parte. Realmente, ainda não tive cabeça para nada. Agradeço imenso a sua disponibilidade.
nodes.npc_translation: That's very kind of you. Honestly, I haven't had the head for anything yet. I really appreciate your availability.

options.text: Não tem de quê, D. Laura. Desabafar também ajuda, se precisar de falar. Estou por cá.
options.translation: You're welcome, D. Laura. Venting also helps, if you need to talk. I'm around.
options.feedback: This offers emotional support, which is very thoughtful. It shows you're not just offering practical help but also a listening ear.

options.text: Pois, imagino. A vida continua, não é? É preciso ter força.
options.translation: Yes, I imagine. Life goes on, doesn't it? One needs to be strong.
options.feedback: This is a common, but sometimes unhelpful, platitude. It might unintentionally dismiss her grief. Better to focus on her feelings directly.

nodes.npc_text: Muito obrigada por ter vindo. Faça o seu caminho. Adeus.
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you very much for coming. Go on your way. Goodbye.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigada, querida. É bom saber. Vou pensar nisso. Obrigada por tudo.
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you, dear. It's good to know. I'll think about it. Thank you for everything.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, é verdade. Mas é difícil. Enfim... Obrigada por ter vindo.
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, that's true. But it's hard. Anyway... Thank you for coming.

dialogues.title: At the Bank Counter
dialogues.context: A customer is at the bank counter, needing to withdraw a large sum and clarify a transaction.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Bom dia, o senhor podia ajudar-me, se faz favor? Precisava de levantar um bocado de dinheiro e confirmar uma transferência.
lines.translation: Good morning, could you help me, please? I needed to withdraw some money and confirm a transfer.

lines.speaker: Bank Clerk
lines.text: Bom dia. Claro, diga. Quanto queria levantar e qual a referência da transferência?
lines.translation: Good morning. Of course, tell me. How much would you like to withdraw and what's the transfer reference?

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: São duzentos euros, por favor. E a transferência é aquela que fiz na semana passada, para a imobiliária. O valor era mil e quinhentos.
lines.translation: It's two hundred euros, please. And the transfer is the one I made last week, to the real estate agency. The amount was one thousand five hundred.

lines.speaker: Bank Clerk
lines.text: Pois, estou a ver. Está tudo em ordem com a transferência. E aqui tem os duzentos euros. Precisa de mais alguma coisa?
lines.translation: Right, I see. Everything is in order with the transfer. And here are your two hundred euros. Do you need anything else?

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: P'ra já, é tudo. Muito obrigado pela ajuda!
lines.translation: For now, that's all. Thank you very much for your help!

dialogues.title: At the Market Stall
dialogues.context: A customer is buying vegetables from a local vendor at the market.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Bom dia! Tem umas semilhas boas hoje?
lines.translation: Good morning! Do you have good potatoes today?

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Bom dia! Tenho sim, fresquinhas da terra. Queres um quilo ou dois?
lines.translation: Good morning! Yes, I do, fresh from the land. Do you want a kilo or two?

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Dê-me um quilo, se faz favor. E um molho de couves, que estas parecem ótimas.
lines.translation: Give me a kilo, please. And a bunch of kale, these look great.

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Pois é, estas couves são mesmo boas. Mais alguma coisa, meu caro?
lines.translation: Indeed, this kale is really good. Anything else, my dear?

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Não, tá bom assim. Quanto é que fica tudo?
lines.translation: No, that's good. How much is everything?

lines.speaker: Vendor
lines.text: Fica três euros e cinquenta. Obrigado e volta sempre!
lines.translation: That's three euros fifty. Thank you and come back anytime!

cultural_notes.title: Navigating Public Services (Finanças)
cultural_notes.body: Dealing with public services like Finanças (the tax office) in Portugal often requires patience and a polite, formal approach. It's common to use 'o senhor/a senhora' even if the official is younger, especially when initiating an interaction. Be prepared to wait, and always have your NIF (Número de Identificação Fiscal) and an identification document (like a passport or residence card) ready. Questions might be indirect, and answers can sometimes be vague, requiring persistence and clear, concise follow-up questions.

cultural_notes.title: Market Interactions and Localisms
cultural_notes.body: When shopping at local markets in Madeira, it's common to hear and use regional words like 'semilha' for potato. Interactions are generally informal and friendly ('tu' form), especially with regular vendors. Don't be surprised by common interjections like 'Pois é!' (Indeed!) or 'Meu caro/minha cara' (My dear) as terms of endearment or friendly address. It's a great opportunity to practice natural, conversational Portuguese.

roleplay.scenario: You are at the Finanças office in Funchal to clarify a notification you received about a property tax payment. You need to understand what the notification means and what action you need to take.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia. Em que posso ajudá-lo/a?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning. How can I help you?

options.text: Bom dia. Recebi uma notificação sobre o IMI e não percebi bem o que devo fazer.
options.translation: Good morning. I received a notification about IMI (property tax) and I didn't quite understand what I need to do.
options.feedback: Excellent start! You clearly state your problem and use a polite, indirect phrasing.

options.text: Olá. Vim cá por causa de um papel das Finanças.
options.translation: Hi. I came here because of a paper from Finanças.
options.feedback: This is a bit too informal and vague for Finanças. Try to be more specific and polite.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, mas que papel é que é? Tem a referência consigo?
nodes.npc_translation: Right, but what paper is it? Do you have the reference with you?

options.text: Ah, sim, peço desculpa. É uma notificação sobre o IMI. Não sei se é para pagar ou só informativo.
options.translation: Ah, yes, I'm sorry. It's a notification about IMI. I don't know if it's for payment or just informative.
options.feedback: Good recovery! You've provided more detail and apologized for the initial vagueness.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Consegue dar-me o seu NIF e mostrar-me a notificação, se faz favor?
nodes.npc_translation: Okay. Can you give me your NIF and show me the notification, please?

options.text: Claro, aqui tem o meu NIF e a notificação. É esta aqui, com a data de há duas semanas.
options.translation: Of course, here's my NIF and the notification. It's this one, dated two weeks ago.
options.feedback: Perfect! You've provided the requested information clearly and promptly.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, estou a ver. Esta notificação é apenas um aviso de que a segunda prestação do IMI vai ser emitida em breve. Não é para pagar já. Apenas para o/a informar.
nodes.npc_translation: Right, I see. This notification is just a warning that the second installment of IMI will be issued soon. It's not for immediate payment. Just to inform you.

options.text: Ah, entendi. Então não preciso de fazer nada agora. Muito obrigado pelo esclarecimento!
options.translation: Ah, I understand. So I don't need to do anything now. Thank you very much for the clarification!
options.feedback: Great! You confirmed your understanding and thanked the official appropriately.

options.text: Mas então, quando é que tenho de pagar? E como?
options.translation: But then, when do I have to pay? And how?
options.feedback: Good follow-up questions. It's important to clarify all doubts.

nodes.npc_text: A data limite para pagamento virá no próximo documento, que deverá receber daqui a umas semanas. Pode pagar em qualquer multibanco ou nas Finanças, como habitualmente.
nodes.npc_translation: The payment deadline will be on the next document, which you should receive in a few weeks. You can pay at any ATM or at Finanças, as usual.

options.text: Ok, muito obrigado. Fico mais tranquilo/a assim. Tenha um bom dia!
options.translation: Okay, thank you very much. I feel more at ease now. Have a good day!
options.feedback: Excellent! You've successfully navigated the interaction and expressed gratitude.

nodes.npc_text: De nada, bom dia e volte sempre que precisar.
nodes.npc_translation: You're welcome, good day and come back whenever you need.

dialogues.title: Briefing the Cleaner
dialogues.context: The owner is meeting the cleaner at the property before the next guests arrive.

lines.speaker: Proprietário
lines.text: Olá, D. Fátima. Então, a casa está pronta p'ra a limpeza.
lines.translation: Hello, D. Fátima. So, the house is ready for cleaning.

lines.speaker: D. Fátima
lines.text: Bom dia, Sr. Miguel. Pois, já vi que sim. Os hóspedes saíram há pouco, certo?
lines.translation: Good morning, Sr. Miguel. Yes, I see. The guests left a little while ago, right?

lines.speaker: Proprietário
lines.text: Exato. E os próximos chegam às quatro da tarde. É preciso caprichar, como sempre.
lines.translation: Exactly. And the next ones arrive at four in the afternoon. It needs to be spotless, as always.

lines.speaker: D. Fátima
lines.text: Não se preocupe, Sr. Miguel. Em três horas está tudo a brilhar. Há alguma coisa específica?
lines.translation: Don't worry, Sr. Miguel. In three hours everything will be sparkling. Is there anything specific?

lines.speaker: Proprietário
lines.text: Ah, sim. O chuveiro do quarto principal está um bocado lento a escoar. Veja se consegue desentupir um pouco.
lines.translation: Ah, yes. The shower in the main bedroom is a bit slow to drain. See if you can unblock it a little.

lines.speaker: D. Fátima
lines.text: Certo, vou ver. P'ra já!
lines.translation: Right, I'll check. See you later!

dialogues.title: Guest Check-in
dialogues.context: The owner is checking in new guests at the property.

lines.speaker: Proprietário
lines.text: Boa tarde! Bem-vindos à Madeira! São o Sr. e a Sra. Silva, certo?
lines.translation: Good afternoon! Welcome to Madeira! You are Mr. and Mrs. Silva, right?

lines.speaker: Hóspede (Sr. Silva)
lines.text: Isso mesmo! Boa tarde. Muito obrigado por nos receber.
lines.translation: That's right! Good afternoon. Thank you very much for having us.

lines.speaker: Proprietário
lines.text: De nada! A viagem correu bem? A chave está aqui, na caixa de segurança ao lado da porta.
lines.translation: You're welcome! Did the journey go well? The key is here, in the safe box next to the door.

lines.speaker: Hóspede (Sra. Silva)
lines.text: Sim, correu tudo bem. Que vista fantástica! É ainda melhor ao vivo.
lines.translation: Yes, everything went well. What a fantastic view! It's even better in person.

lines.speaker: Proprietário
lines.text: Pois é! É um dos pontos fortes da casa. Vou só explicar-vos rapidamente o funcionamento da televisão e do Wi-Fi.
lines.translation: Indeed! It's one of the house's strong points. I'll just quickly explain how the TV and Wi-Fi work.

lines.speaker: Hóspede (Sr. Silva)
lines.text: Perfeito. E se tivermos alguma dúvida durante a estadia?
lines.translation: Perfect. And if we have any questions during our stay?

lines.speaker: Proprietário
lines.text: Podem ligar-me ou enviar mensagem a qualquer altura. O meu número está no frigorífico.
lines.translation: You can call me or send a message anytime. My number is on the fridge.

cultural_notes.title: Directness with Service Providers
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, as in mainland Portugal, communication with service providers like cleaners or plumbers tends to be direct and focused on the task. While politeness is always valued, there's no need for excessive preamble. Clearly stating the issue or the task at hand is efficient and appreciated. Using 'D. Fátima' or 'Sr. Miguel' (Dona/Senhor + first name) is a common, respectful way to address people in these roles when there's a recurring professional relationship.

cultural_notes.title: Managing Noise and Neighbour Relations
cultural_notes.body: Living in close quarters, especially in residential areas, means being mindful of noise. It's common courtesy to inform neighbours if there might be unusual noise (e.g., a repair, a small gathering) or to apologize if noise has occurred. This helps maintain good community relations. Phrases like 'Peço desculpa pelo barulho' (I apologize for the noise) or 'Desculpe o incómodo' (Sorry for the inconvenience) are important for showing respect and consideration.

roleplay.scenario: A guest has just called you, the property owner, to report a plumbing issue: the toilet flush (autoclismo) in one of the bathrooms isn't working properly. You need to gather more information, reassure the guest, and arrange for a plumber.

nodes.npc_text: Olá, sou eu, o proprietário. Ligou-me a dizer que há um problema, certo? Diga lá o que se passa.
nodes.npc_translation: Hello, it's me, the owner. You called me to say there's a problem, right? Tell me what's happening.

options.text: Sim, o autoclismo da casa de banho do quarto principal não está a funcionar bem.
options.translation: Yes, the toilet flush in the main bedroom's bathroom isn't working well.
options.feedback: Good, you clearly stated the problem. Now to ask for details.

options.text: Há um problema com a água na casa de banho.
options.translation: There's a problem with the water in the bathroom.
options.feedback: This is a bit vague. Try to be more specific to help the owner understand the issue quickly.

nodes.npc_text: Certo, mas pode ser mais específico? O que é que se passa exatamente com a água?
nodes.npc_translation: Okay, but can you be more specific? What exactly is happening with the water?

options.text: O autoclismo da casa de banho principal não está a deitar água suficiente.
options.translation: The flush in the main bathroom isn't releasing enough water.
options.feedback: Much better, clear and specific. Good job clarifying!

nodes.npc_text: Ah, entendi. O autoclismo não está a encher ou não deita água? É importante para eu saber qual o canalizador a chamar.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, I see. Is the flush not filling or not releasing water? It's important for me to know which plumber to call.

options.text: Ele enche, mas quando carregamos, a água não desce com força. Parece entupido.
options.translation: It fills, but when we press, the water doesn't flush with force. It seems blocked.
options.feedback: Excellent detail. This helps the owner diagnose and dispatch the right help.

options.text: Não sei bem, só sei que não funciona.
options.translation: I don't really know, I just know it doesn't work.
options.feedback: Try to provide as much detail as possible, even if you're not an expert. What do you observe?

nodes.npc_text: Compreendo. Consegue ver se está a vazar água para fora ou se o problema é apenas dentro da sanita?
nodes.npc_translation: I understand. Can you see if water is leaking outside or if the problem is just inside the toilet bowl?

options.text: Não, não está a vazar. Apenas a água não desce com força na sanita.
options.translation: No, it's not leaking. Just the water doesn't flush with force in the toilet.
options.feedback: Good, that's crucial information. Now the owner knows it's likely an internal blockage.

nodes.npc_text: Muito obrigado pela informação. Vou já contactar o canalizador. Ele deve conseguir ir aí dentro de uma hora. Posso dar-lhe a chave ou prefere que ele bata à porta?
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you very much for the information. I'll contact the plumber right away. He should be able to get there within an hour. Can I give him the key or do you prefer he knocks on the door?

options.text: Não se preocupe, estamos cá. Ele pode bater à porta.
options.translation: Don't worry, we're here. He can knock on the door.
options.feedback: Good, you've confirmed your availability. This simplifies things for the owner and plumber.

options.text: Se puder dar-lhe a chave, agradeço. Vamos sair por um bocado.
options.translation: If you can give him the key, I'd appreciate it. We're going out for a bit.
options.feedback: This is a valid option, showing flexibility. Make sure the owner knows how to access it.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Nesse caso, vou deixar a chave na caixa de segurança e dar-lhe o código. O canalizador ligar-lhe-á quando estiver a caminho. Peço desculpa pelo incómodo.
nodes.npc_translation: Right. In that case, I'll leave the key in the safe box and give him the code. The plumber will call you when he's on his way. I apologize for the inconvenience.

nodes.npc_text: Perfeito. Vou avisar o Sr. João que pode bater à porta. Ele é muito eficiente. Peço desculpa pelo incómodo e obrigado pela compreensão.
nodes.npc_translation: Perfect. I'll let Sr. João know he can knock on the door. He's very efficient. I apologize for the inconvenience and thank you for your understanding.

dialogues.title: Ferry Cancelled to Porto Santo
dialogues.context: Ana and Pedro were planning a weekend trip to Porto Santo, but the ferry 'Lobo Marinho' was cancelled due to bad weather. They are now at a café in Funchal, discussing alternatives.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Não acredito! O Lobo Marinho foi cancelado outra vez? Que chatice!
lines.translation: I don't believe it! The Lobo Marinho was cancelled again? What a bother!

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: Pois é, Ana. Por causa do mar agitado. É um imprevisto, mas temos de contornar.
lines.translation: That's right, Ana. Because of the rough sea. It's an unforeseen event, but we have to get around it.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: E agora? O que é que fazemos com o fim de semana? Já tínhamos tudo planeado para Porto Santo.
lines.translation: And now? What do we do with the weekend? We already had everything planned for Porto Santo.

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: Não há problema, encontramos uma alternativa. Que tal explorarmos o norte da ilha? Podíamos ir a São Vicente ou Santana.
lines.translation: No problem, we'll find an alternative. How about exploring the north of the island? We could go to São Vicente or Santana.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Hum... é uma ideia. Mas e o alojamento? Já tínhamos reservado o de Porto Santo.
lines.translation: Hmm... it's an idea. But what about the accommodation? We had already booked the one in Porto Santo.

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: Ligamos para lá e tentamos cancelar sem custos. Para o norte, podemos ver um alojamento local de última hora. É sempre mais fácil.
lines.translation: We'll call them and try to cancel without cost. For the north, we can look for last-minute local accommodation. It's always easier.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Está bem, Pedro. É o que temos. Pelo menos não ficamos em casa. O que achas de irmos ver as grutas de São Vicente?
lines.translation: Okay, Pedro. It is what it is. At least we won't stay home. What do you think of going to see the São Vicente caves?

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: Excelente ideia! E depois um almoço com espetada e bolo do caco por lá? Fica combinado.
lines.translation: Excellent idea! And then lunch with espetada and bolo do caco there? It's a plan.

cultural_notes.title: Flexibility is Key in Island Life
cultural_notes.body: Living on an island, especially one with a dynamic climate like Madeira, often requires a high degree of flexibility. Weather conditions can frequently impact travel plans, whether it's ferry cancellations to Porto Santo, road closures due to landslides (derrocadas), or even flight delays. Locals are accustomed to these 'imprevistos' and often have a pragmatic approach, quickly brainstorming alternatives rather than dwelling on the disruption. Phrases like 'É o que temos' (It is what it is) or 'Dá-se a volta' (We'll find a way around it) reflect this adaptability.

cultural_notes.title: Negotiating Alternatives Politely
cultural_notes.body: When plans collapse, the way you propose and negotiate alternatives is important. While directness is generally appreciated in Portugal, softening suggestions with phrases like 'O que achas de...?' (What do you think of...?), 'Talvez pudéssemos...' (Perhaps we could...), or expressing understanding ('Isso complica um bocado...') helps maintain a positive atmosphere. Avoid sounding demanding or overly critical of the changed circumstances. A calm, problem-solving attitude is valued.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a café in Funchal, waiting for your friends, Marta and Tiago, who are driving from the north of the island to meet you for lunch. You receive a message from the café owner that a main road is closed due to a small landslide, and your friends might be delayed or need a new route. You need to call them and figure out a new plan.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Desculpe interromper, mas recebi agora uma notícia: a estrada para o Faial está cortada. Os seus amigos vinham de lá, não era?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! Sorry to interrupt, but I just got news: the road to Faial is closed. Your friends were coming from there, weren't they?

options.text: Ah, não! Que percalço! Sim, vinham do Faial. Vou ligar-lhes já.
options.translation: Oh, no! What a setback! Yes, they were coming from Faial. I'll call them now.
options.feedback: Good, you acknowledged the problem and stated your immediate action. 'Percalço' is a great word here.

options.text: Cortada? Isso complica tudo. Mas eles devem ter um plano B, não?
options.translation: Closed? That complicates everything. But they must have a Plan B, right?
options.feedback: You expressed concern and a slight expectation for them to solve it. Now, take action by calling them.

nodes.npc_text: (Você liga para a Marta. Ela atende, um bocado aflita.) Marta: Olá! Estamos parados, a estrada está mesmo cortada. Não sabemos o que fazer, estamos a ver no GPS.
nodes.npc_translation: (You call Marta. She answers, a bit stressed.) Marta: Hello! We're stopped, the road is really closed. We don't know what to do, we're checking the GPS.

options.text: Pois, o senhor do café avisou-me. Que tal darem a volta pela Ribeira Brava e virmos ter a um sítio mais a meio?
options.translation: Yeah, the café owner told me. How about you go around via Ribeira Brava and we meet somewhere more in the middle?
options.feedback: Excellent, you're proposing a concrete alternative route and meeting point. This shows initiative.

options.text: Calma, Marta. Há sempre uma solução. Conseguem ir por outra estrada, mesmo que seja mais longa?
options.translation: Calm down, Marta. There's always a solution. Can you take another road, even if it's longer?
options.feedback: Good, you're reassuring your friend and prompting them to think about alternatives. Now, be ready to suggest one.

nodes.npc_text: (Marta e Tiago conversam. Marta volta ao telemóvel.) Marta: Sim, o Tiago está a ver. Pela Ribeira Brava é capaz de demorar mais uma hora. Onde é que nos encontramos então?
nodes.npc_translation: (Marta and Tiago talk. Marta gets back on the phone.) Marta: Yes, Tiago is checking. Via Ribeira Brava might take another hour. Where should we meet then?

options.text: Que tal irmos todos almoçar à Taberna da Poncha, em São Vicente? Fica mais perto para vocês e é sempre bom.
options.translation: How about we all go for lunch at Taberna da Poncha, in São Vicente? It's closer for you and it's always good.
options.feedback: Great, you've chosen a specific, well-known Madeiran spot that's convenient for them. This is good local knowledge.

options.text: Se demoram uma hora, talvez seja melhor almoçarmos mais para o oeste. Podíamos ir a um sítio em Câmara de Lobos?
options.translation: If it takes an hour, maybe it's better to have lunch further west. Could we go to a place in Câmara de Lobos?
options.feedback: Good, you're adapting the plan based on the new travel time and suggesting a new area. Be ready to name a specific place.

nodes.npc_text: (Marta fala com Tiago novamente.) Marta: Boa ideia! A Taberna da Poncha é ótima. Então, vamos para lá. Chegamos em cerca de uma hora e meia, talvez. Vemo-nos lá!
nodes.npc_translation: (Marta talks to Tiago again.) Marta: Good idea! Taberna da Poncha is great. So, we're heading there. We'll arrive in about an hour and a half, maybe. See you there!

options.text: Combinado! Vou avisar o senhor do café que mudámos de planos. Até já!
options.translation: Agreed! I'll let the café owner know we've changed plans. See you soon!
options.feedback: Perfect! You've confirmed the plan and remembered to inform the service staff, showing good social awareness. This is a solid resolution.

dialogues.title: Café Chaos
dialogues.context: You're at a very busy café in Funchal during peak hours, trying to order. The barista is fast, and there's a lot of background noise.

lines.speaker: Barista
lines.text: Próximo! O que vai ser? Temos imensa gente hoje, despachem-se!
lines.translation: Next! What will it be? We have loads of people today, hurry up!

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Desculpe, não percebi bem. Pode repetir, por favor?
lines.translation: Sorry, I didn't quite understand. Can you repeat, please?

lines.speaker: Barista
lines.text: Disse 'o que vai ser'! Bolo do caco, bica, poncha? O que é?
lines.translation: I said 'what will it be'! Bolo do caco, espresso, poncha? What is it?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Ah, pois, sim! Queria um bolo do caco com manteiga de alho e uma bica cheia, por favor.
lines.translation: Ah, yes, right! I'd like a bolo do caco with garlic butter and a full espresso, please.

lines.speaker: Barista
lines.text: Certo. Um bolo do caco com alho e uma bica. Mais alguma coisa?
lines.translation: Right. A bolo do caco with garlic and an espresso. Anything else?

dialogues.title: Bad Phone Line
dialogues.context: You're on the phone trying to confirm details for a delivery, but the line quality is very poor.

lines.speaker: Funcionário
lines.text: A sua encomenda... uhm... será entregue... (chiado)... na terça-feira.
lines.translation: Your order... uhm... will be delivered... (static)... on Tuesday.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Desculpe, a linha está um bocado má. Não percebi a data. É na terça?
lines.translation: Sorry, the line is a bit bad. I didn't catch the date. Is it on Tuesday?

lines.speaker: Funcionário
lines.text: Sim, na terça-feira... (ruído)... entre as nove e o meio-dia.
lines.translation: Yes, on Tuesday... (noise)... between nine and midday.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Pois, percebi. Entre as nove e o meio-dia, então. Obrigado!
lines.translation: Yes, I understood. Between nine and midday, then. Thank you!

cultural_notes.title: Asking for Clarification Politely
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, when you don't understand something, especially due to noise or fast speech, it's common and polite to say 'Não percebi bem' (I didn't quite understand) or 'Pode repetir, por favor?' (Can you repeat, please?). In very informal contexts, a short 'Diga?' or 'Como?' can also be used, similar to 'Pardon?' or 'What?' in English. Avoid using 'você' for direct polite address; instead, use the third person verb form or 'o senhor / a senhora'.

cultural_notes.title: The Versatility of 'Pois' and 'Pois é'
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois' and 'Pois é' are very common interjections in European Portuguese. 'Pois' can mean 'yes', 'of course', 'that's right', or simply acknowledge what someone has said. 'Pois é' is similar, often used to confirm something already known, express agreement, or sometimes a slight resignation. They are excellent fillers and markers of active listening in conversation.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a very busy 'pastelaria' (bakery/cafe) in Funchal. You want to order a specific type of bolo do caco and a drink, but the place is packed, and the person serving you is speaking quickly and a bit indistinctly.

nodes.npc_text: Próximo! Diga lá, o que é que vai ser? Temos imensa gente hoje, não consigo ouvir nada!

options.text: Olá, queria um bolo do caco com manteiga de alho e uma bica, por favor.

options.text: Desculpe, não percebi bem. Pode repetir?
options.translation: Sorry, I didn't quite understand. Can you repeat?
options.feedback: Excellent, you immediately asked for clarification. This is a key skill in noisy environments.

nodes.npc_text: Disse 'Próximo, o que vai ser?' Há muita confusão, não é? O que é que quer?
nodes.npc_translation: I said 'Next, what will it be?' It's very confusing, isn't it? What do you want?

options.text: Sim, é verdade. Queria um bolo do caco com manteiga de alho e uma bica.
options.translation: Yes, it's true. I'd like a bolo do caco with garlic butter and an espresso.
options.feedback: Well done, you understood and placed your order.

nodes.npc_text: Um bolo do caco com manteiga de alho e uma... o quê? Não ouvi a bebida, o barulho é imenso!
nodes.npc_translation: A bolo do caco with garlic butter and a... what? I didn't hear the drink, the noise is immense!

options.text: Uma bica! Uma bica normal, por favor.
options.translation: An espresso! A normal espresso, please.
options.feedback: You repeated the item clearly. Good job focusing on the specific missing information.

options.text: Desculpe, é uma bica. A fila está enorme, não consigo ouvir bem também.
options.translation: Sorry, it's an espresso. The queue is huge, I can't hear well either.
options.feedback: You clarified and showed empathy for the situation, which is good social interaction.

nodes.npc_text: Certo, então é um bolo do caco com manteiga de alho e uma bica. É isso? Mais alguma coisa?
nodes.npc_translation: Right, so it's a bolo do caco with garlic butter and an espresso. Is that it? Anything else?

options.text: Não, obrigado. É só isso.
options.translation: No, thank you. That's all.
options.feedback: Clear and concise. You confirmed the order and finished the interaction.

options.text: Pois, sim, é isso mesmo. Obrigado!
options.translation: Yes, that's right. Thank you!
options.feedback: Using 'Pois, sim' is very natural for confirmation in Portugal. Well done.

nodes.npc_text: São três euros e cinquenta, por favor. O seu pedido fica ali no balcão, à espera.

dialogues.title: Telling Your Madeira Story
dialogues.context: At a local café, a new acquaintance, Sofia, asks about your journey to Madeira.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Então, conte-me lá, como é que veio parar à Madeira? Já cá mora há muito tempo?
lines.translation: So, tell me, how did you end up in Madeira? Have you been living here long?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Pois, cheguei há uns cinco anos. Vim p'ra cá por causa de uma oportunidade de trabalho, mas o que me fez ficar foi mesmo o ritmo de vida e a natureza.
lines.translation: Well, I arrived about five years ago. I came here because of a job opportunity, but what made me stay was really the pace of life and the nature.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Ah, pois é, isso acontece com muitos! A ilha tem um encanto especial. E como se sente agora, já se sente um madeirense?
lines.translation: Ah, that's right, that happens to many! The island has a special charm. And how do you feel now, do you already feel Madeiran?

lines.speaker: Learner
lines.text: Olhe, sinto-me um madeirense de coração, sem dúvida! A ilha mudou a minha perspetiva sobre o que é ter qualidade de vida. E já não troco o bolo do caco por nada!
lines.translation: Look, I feel Madeiran at heart, no doubt! The island changed my perspective on what it means to have quality of life. And I wouldn't trade bolo do caco for anything now!

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Ah, isso é que é bom de ouvir! Sinal que está mesmo integrado. É sempre um prazer ver quem se apaixona pela nossa ilha.
lines.translation: Ah, that's good to hear! A sign that you're truly integrated. It's always a pleasure to see those who fall in love with our island.

cultural_notes.title: Expressing Attachment to Madeira
cultural_notes.body: Madeirans are generally very proud of their island and appreciate when foreigners express genuine affection and integration. Using phrases like 'madeirense de coração' (Madeiran at heart) or mentioning specific local elements like 'as levadas' or 'o bolo do caco' can show a deeper connection and appreciation, often leading to a warmer reception. It's common to share personal anecdotes about how the island has positively impacted one's life.

cultural_notes.title: The Role of Humour and Connectors
cultural_notes.body: In casual conversations, a touch of self-deprecating humour or lightheartedness is common and can help build rapport. Using connectors like 'Pois', 'Então', 'Olhe', 'Sem dúvida' makes your speech sound more natural and fluent, characteristic of L5 (B2) level. These words help to structure your thoughts and signal your engagement in the conversation.

roleplay.scenario: You're at a community event, and a local resident, João, asks about your experience living in Madeira. He's curious about what you do and how you've adapted.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Não me lembro de o ver por cá antes. É novo na ilha, ou já cá mora há algum tempo? O que o trouxe à nossa pérola do Atlântico?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! I don't remember seeing you around before. Are you new to the island, or have you been living here for a while? What brought you to our Pearl of the Atlantic?

options.text: Já cá estou há uns bons anos, João. Vim pela aventura, e acabei por ficar pela paz e beleza da ilha.
options.translation: I've been here for a good few years, João. I came for the adventure, and ended up staying for the peace and beauty of the island.
options.feedback: Excellent start! You've directly answered his questions and added a personal touch.

options.text: Cheguei há pouco tempo, mas já estou a adorar tudo! Vim de férias e decidi que queria mudar de vida.
options.translation: I arrived recently, but I'm already loving everything! I came on holiday and decided I wanted to change my life.
options.feedback: Good, but you could elaborate a bit more on what specifically made you stay, linking to the island's charm.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, a Madeira tem esse poder! E o que faz por cá? Conseguiu adaptar-se ao nosso ritmo, um bocado mais lento que o do continente, não é?
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, Madeira has that power! And what do you do here? Have you managed to adapt to our pace, a bit slower than the mainland's, right?

options.text: Sou professor, e sim, o ritmo de vida aqui é mais calmo, o que me permite desfrutar mais da natureza e das pessoas. Sinto-me bastante integrado.
options.translation: I'm a teacher, and yes, the pace of life here is calmer, which allows me to enjoy nature and people more. I feel quite integrated.
options.feedback: Perfect! You've answered directly, mentioned your profession without an article, and used 'integrado'.

options.text: Trabalho remotamente, o que é ótimo para aproveitar a ilha. O ritmo é diferente, mas para melhor, sem dúvida!
options.translation: I work remotely, which is great for enjoying the island. The pace is different, but for the better, no doubt!
options.feedback: Very good, you've highlighted a common reason for foreigners to move and expressed a positive opinion.

nodes.npc_text: Que coragem! E o que o convenceu a ficar? Houve algum momento em que pensou: 'É aqui que quero viver'?
nodes.npc_translation: What courage! And what convinced you to stay? Was there a moment when you thought: 'This is where I want to live'?

options.text: Sim, foi quando fiz a primeira levada. Aquelas paisagens e a sensação de paz... A ilha mudou a minha perspetiva sobre a vida.
options.translation: Yes, it was when I did my first levada walk. Those landscapes and the feeling of peace... The island changed my perspective on life.
options.feedback: Excellent! You've used a specific Madeiran reference and a phrase pattern about changing perspective.

options.text: Acho que foi a hospitalidade das pessoas, e claro, a poncha! Sinto-me em casa aqui.
options.translation: I think it was the hospitality of the people, and of course, the poncha! I feel at home here.
options.feedback: Great use of local cultural references and expressing a feeling of belonging.

nodes.npc_text: Fico contente por ouvir isso! É bom ter gente que valoriza o que temos. E qual é a sua coisa favorita na Madeira, depois de todos estes anos?
nodes.npc_translation: I'm glad to hear that! It's good to have people who value what we have. And what's your favorite thing about Madeira, after all these years?

options.text: Diria que é a mistura de natureza exuberante com a simpatia das pessoas. É uma combinação única que me faz sentir um verdadeiro madeirense de coração.
options.translation: I'd say it's the mix of lush nature with the kindness of the people. It's a unique combination that makes me feel like a true Madeiran at heart.
options.feedback: Perfect! You've combined several positive aspects and used a key phrase pattern. Conversation ends naturally here.

options.text: Sem dúvida, as levadas e o bolo do caco! E o facto de poder ir à praia e à montanha no mesmo dia. É fantástico!
options.translation: Without a doubt, the levadas and the bolo do caco! And the fact that I can go to the beach and the mountains on the same day. It's fantastic!
options.feedback: Excellent! You've used specific Madeiran references and expressed enthusiasm. Conversation ends naturally here.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é! É isso mesmo que nos prende à ilha. Fico feliz que se sinta assim. Bem-vindo à família madeirense!
nodes.npc_translation: That's right! That's exactly what ties us to the island. I'm happy you feel that way. Welcome to the Madeiran family!

options.text: Muito obrigado, João! É um prazer fazer parte dela.
options.translation: Thank you very much, João! It's a pleasure to be a part of it.
options.feedback: A warm and appropriate closing. Well done!

options.text: Obrigado! É um sentimento recíproco.
options.translation: Thanks! The feeling is mutual.
options.feedback: A concise and polite response showing mutual appreciation. Good job!

mission.title: Share Your Madeira Story at a Social Gathering
prep: Mentally prepare a 1-2 minute 'pitch' about your journey to Madeira, what you do, and how the island has impacted you.
Practise using at least two `phrase_patterns` from this lesson in your story.
Think of specific Madeiran details (e.g., levadas, bolo do caco, poncha) to include naturally.
Rehearse expressing your feelings of integration and appreciation for the island.
fallback_phrases: Peço desculpa, não percebi bem. Pode repetir, por favor?
Ah, interessante! Pode explicar um bocado melhor?
Não tenho a certeza de como expressar isso em português ainda.
likely_responses: Ah, que interessante! E o que o fez ficar?
Gosta mesmo da ilha, não é? Dá para ver!
E o que faz por cá? Conseguiu adaptar-se bem?
É bom ouvir isso! A ilha tem um encanto especial.

goals: Articulate your personal story of living in Madeira with confidence and detail.
Use appropriate connectors and descriptive language to enhance your narrative.
Express feelings and opinions about the island, including its impact on your life, with nuance.
Engage in a natural and extended conversation about your integration into local life, demonstrating a B2 level of fluency.

situation.title: The Long Conversation
situation.summary: An evening of unscripted talk: sustaining a multi-topic conversation for an hour — stories, opinions, jokes, disagreements and plans — as an equal at the table.

phrase_patterns.translation: By the way, I was thinking about what you told me about {topic}...

slots.description: a subject previously discussed or mentioned
options: a receita do bolo do caco

variants.text: Sabes, fiquei a pensar no que me disseste sobre {topic}...
variants.translation: You know, I kept thinking about what you told me about {topic}...

phrase_patterns.base: Concordo contigo, mas por outro lado, também acho que {alternative_view}.
phrase_patterns.translation: I agree with you, but on the other hand, I also think that {alternative_view}.

slots.name: alternative_view
options: a situação é mais complexa do que parece
depende muito do ponto de vista de cada um

variants.note: More formal way to express disagreement

vocabulary.translation: to vent, to get something off one's chest
vocabulary.note: Common in conversations about personal feelings
Often used in more structured discussions

Common expression for shared humour
Useful for navigating delicate subjects

dialogues.title: Evening Chat at the Café
dialogues.context: At a local café in Funchal, two friends, Ana and Carlos, are catching up after work, discussing a recent local event.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Então, Carlos, o que achaste daquele festival de folclore no fim de semana? Fui lá e achei um bocado confuso, p'ra ser sincera.
lines.translation: So, Carlos, what did you think of that folklore festival at the weekend? I went there and found it a bit confusing, to be honest.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Pois é, Ana, percebo o que dizes. Eu também fui. As danças eram bonitas, sim, mas a organização... ui, parecia que ninguém sabia bem o que estava a fazer. Mas os trajes, esses estavam impecáveis!
lines.translation: Well, Ana, I understand what you're saying. I went too. The dances were beautiful, yes, but the organization... oh, it seemed like no one really knew what they were doing. But the costumes, those were impeccable!

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Exato! Os trajes eram lindos. E a comida, provaste alguma coisa? Aquelas espetadas estavam divinais, não achas?
lines.translation: Exactly! The costumes were beautiful. And the food, did you try anything? Those espetadas were divine, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Ah, as espetadas! Claro que sim! Comi umas duas, estavam mesmo no ponto. É daquelas coisas que nos fazem esquecer a confusão toda, não é?
lines.translation: Ah, the espetadas! Of course! I ate about two, they were perfectly cooked. It's one of those things that makes us forget all the confusion, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: É verdade. Mas, por acaso, estava a pensar no que me disseste sobre a nova política de resíduos. Achas que vai mesmo funcionar aqui na ilha?
lines.translation: It's true. But, by the way, I was thinking about what you told me about the new waste policy. Do you really think it will work here on the island?

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Hum, é um assunto melindroso, Ana. Tenho as minhas dúvidas, p'ra ser honesto. Vai depender muito da colaboração de todos. Mas temos de tentar, não é? É pelo bem da nossa ilha.
lines.translation: Hmm, it's a sensitive topic, Ana. I have my doubts, to be honest. It will depend a lot on everyone's cooperation. But we have to try, don't we? It's for the good of our island.

cultural_notes.title: Navigating Disagreement in Portuguese Conversations
cultural_notes.body: In Portuguese culture, especially in social settings, direct confrontation is often avoided. When expressing disagreement, it's common to preface your opinion with phrases that acknowledge the other person's point of view, such as 'Percebo o que dizes' (I understand what you're saying) or 'Concordo contigo, mas...' (I agree with you, but...). This softens the impact and maintains harmony. Humour can also be used to defuse tension or gently challenge an idea without causing offence.

cultural_notes.title: The Art of Storytelling and Anecdotes
cultural_notes.body: Sharing personal stories and anecdotes is a vital part of social interaction in Madeira. It's a way to connect, entertain, and illustrate a point. Don't be afraid to link a current topic to a past experience or a funny incident. Phrases like 'Isso faz-me lembrar...' (That reminds me...) are excellent ways to introduce your own narrative and contribute to the 'long conversation'.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a neighbour's house for an informal evening gathering. Your neighbour, Dona Fátima, is known for her lively conversations and strong opinions. She's just brought up a local issue.

nodes.npc_text: Então, o que achas disto dos novos horários dos autocarros na Ponta do Sol? Para mim, é um disparate! Chega a ser ridículo como eles mudam as coisas sem pensar nas pessoas que precisam de trabalhar.
nodes.npc_translation: So, what do you think about these new bus schedules in Ponta do Sol? For me, it's nonsense! It's ridiculous how they change things without thinking about the people who need to work.

options.text: Percebo o teu ponto de vista, Dona Fátima. Realmente, para quem usa o autocarro todos os dias, deve ser complicado.
options.translation: I understand your point of view, Dona Fátima. Indeed, for those who use the bus every day, it must be complicated.
options.feedback: Good job acknowledging her perspective first. Now you can elaborate or offer your own thoughts.

options.text: Eu acho que é uma medida necessária para otimizar os recursos, mas concordo que a comunicação podia ser melhor.
options.translation: I think it's a necessary measure to optimize resources, but I agree that communication could be better.
options.feedback: You've offered a nuanced opinion and found common ground. Be prepared for her response!

nodes.npc_text: Exatamente! E depois, o que acontece? As pessoas chegam atrasadas ao trabalho, ou perdem as consultas. É uma falta de respeito! E tu, usas muito os transportes públicos?
nodes.npc_translation: Exactly! And then what happens? People arrive late for work, or miss appointments. It's a lack of respect! And you, do you use public transport much?

options.text: Eu não uso tanto, mas tenho amigos que dependem deles e queixam-se imenso. Isso faz-me lembrar uma vez que...
options.translation: I don't use it as much, but I have friends who depend on them and complain a lot. That reminds me of a time when...
options.feedback: Excellent! You've linked it to a personal connection and are ready to share an anecdote, keeping the conversation flowing.

options.text: Não, eu costumo andar de carro, mas consigo imaginar a frustração. Talvez devessem ter feito uma consulta pública antes.
options.translation: No, I usually drive, but I can imagine the frustration. Maybe they should have done a public consultation beforehand.
options.feedback: You've offered a solution and shown empathy. This is a good way to contribute constructively.

nodes.npc_text: Otimizar recursos? Mas à custa de quem? As pessoas não são números! Mas sim, a comunicação é sempre um problema. Por falar nisso, já viste as notícias sobre o novo projeto de turismo rural na serra?
nodes.npc_translation: Optimize resources? But at whose expense? People are not numbers! But yes, communication is always a problem. Speaking of which, have you seen the news about the new rural tourism project in the mountains?

options.text: Sim, vi algo. Parece interessante, mas tenho algumas reservas sobre o impacto ambiental.
options.translation: Yes, I saw something. It seems interesting, but I have some reservations about the environmental impact.
options.feedback: You've transitioned to a new topic and offered a thoughtful, slightly critical opinion. Good for L5.

options.text: Não, não vi. Conta-me mais! É perto de alguma levada que conheço?
options.translation: No, I haven't seen it. Tell me more! Is it near a levada I know?
options.feedback: You've shown interest and asked for more details, keeping her engaged. Good for sustaining conversation.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, conta, conta! Adoro uma boa história! Foi algo relacionado com atrasos também?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, tell me, tell me! I love a good story! Was it also related to delays?

options.text: Pois, foi uma vez que o meu amigo ia para o aeroporto e o autocarro avariou a meio caminho. Quase perdia o voo!
options.translation: Well, it was once when my friend was going to the airport and the bus broke down halfway. He almost missed his flight!
options.feedback: A good, relatable anecdote. This ends the specific branch, but the conversation could continue informally.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, isso é que é! Mas eles nunca perguntam a ninguém. É sempre de cima para baixo. Mas olha, não vamos esticar a conversa só sobre isto. Já provaste os bolinhos de mel que fiz?
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, that's it! But they never ask anyone. It's always top-down. But look, let's not prolong the conversation just about this. Have you tried the honey cakes I made?

options.text: Ainda não, mas têm um aspeto delicioso! Adoro bolinhos de mel. Qual é o segredo da tua receita, Dona Fátima?
options.translation: Not yet, but they look delicious! I love honey cakes. What's the secret to your recipe, Dona Fátima?
options.feedback: You've smoothly transitioned to a new, lighter topic and shown interest in her skills. This is excellent social interaction.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, é um assunto melindroso, não é? Por um lado, traz investimento, mas por outro... a natureza é tão frágil. Mas diz-me, o que achas que podiam fazer para mitigar isso?
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, it's a sensitive topic, isn't it? On one hand, it brings investment, but on the other... nature is so fragile. But tell me, what do you think they could do to mitigate that?

options.text: Talvez pudessem implementar mais energias renováveis no projeto e limitar o número de visitantes por dia.
options.translation: Perhaps they could implement more renewable energy in the project and limit the number of visitors per day.
options.feedback: You've offered concrete suggestions, showing critical thinking and problem-solving skills, perfect for L5.

nodes.npc_text: É mais para os lados da Serra de Água, sim. É um conceito de 'glamping', sabes? Tendas de luxo no meio da natureza. Eu acho que é uma boa ideia, p'ra atrair um turismo diferente.
nodes.npc_translation: It's more towards the Serra de Água, yes. It's a 'glamping' concept, you know? Luxury tents in the middle of nature. I think it's a good idea, to attract a different kind of tourism.

options.text: Ah, 'glamping'! Já ouvi falar. Pode ser interessante, sim, mas espero que respeitem a paisagem natural.
options.translation: Ah, 'glamping'! I've heard of it. It could be interesting, yes, but I hope they respect the natural landscape.
options.feedback: You've understood the concept and offered a balanced opinion, showing concern for the environment. Good for L5.

Mas será que isso vai mesmo resultar?
